primitives.name: tabula.plena
primitives.definition: The pre-semantic fullness — the undifferentiated potential from which all structure emerges.

primitives.name: seed.entropy
primitives.definition: The irreducible minimum of disorder required to break symmetry and initiate differentiation.

primitives.name: init.clock
primitives.definition: The activation of sequencing — the primitive that establishes before and after.

primitives.name: init.identity
primitives.definition: The first act of distinction — the capacity to differentiate this from not-this.

primitives.name: init.boundary
primitives.definition: The establishment of inside and outside — the primitive that creates containment and separation.

primitives.name: geometric_vacuum
primitives.definition: The absence of any geometric structure.

primitives.name: node
primitives.definition: The minimal locus of semantic state; precursor to a geometric point.

primitives.name: point
primitives.definition: A single exact location with no size or extension.

primitives.name: line
primitives.definition: The shortest path between two points.

primitives.name: boundary
primitives.definition: A limit separating inside from outside.

primitives.name: membrane
primitives.definition: A flexible boundary capable of filtering or transmitting effects.

primitives.name: direction
primitives.definition: An oriented path along which movement can occur.

primitives.name: well
primitives.definition: A local minimum in geometric or energetic structure.

primitives.name: logical_vacuum
primitives.definition: Absence of any logical state or evaluable condition.

primitives.name: superposition
primitives.definition: Multiple truth states coexist until resolved.

primitives.name: permanently_unresolvable
primitives.definition: Can never be evaluated or resolved.

primitives.name: persistence
primitives.definition: Continuation of a state over time.

primitives.name: availability
primitives.definition: Can be accessed or acted upon.

primitives.name: unavailability
primitives.definition: Cannot be accessed or acted upon.

primitives.name: basin
primitives.definition: Region of state-space toward which dynamics flow.

primitives.name: stability_gradient
primitives.definition: Rate of stability change over time.

primitives.name: surplus_conversion
primitives.definition: Transforming surplus into growth or repair.

primitives.name: duration
primitives.definition: Length of time a sound is sustained.

primitives.name: articulation
primitives.definition: How a note is initiated and released.

primitives.name: fear
primitives.definition: Anticipatory negative state triggered by threat.

primitives.name: anticipatory_resolution
primitives.definition: Waiting for a single outcome among many.

primitives.name: contour
primitives.definition: Shape of semantic movement across time.

primitives.name: cadence
primitives.definition: Pacing pattern governing release of meaning.

primitives.name: symbol
primitives.definition: A unit that stands for something else.

primitives.name: signal
primitives.definition: Information relevant to goals or models.

primitives.name: channel
primitives.definition: Medium through which information is transmitted.

primitives.name: difference
primitives.definition: Two things are not the same.

primitives.name: sameness
primitives.definition: Two things are identical or equivalent.

primitives.name: separation
primitives.definition: Two things apart or not connected.

primitives.name: medium
primitives.definition: Substance or field through which interactions occur.

primitives.name: constraint_source
primitives.definition: External origin of limits or pressures.

primitives.name: comm_channel
primitives.definition: Pathway through which signals or resources are transmitted.

primitives.name: quantum_transition_time
primitives.definition: Time scale of quantum state changes.

primitives.name: transition
primitives.definition: Movement from one state to another.

primitives.name: constraint_pressure
primitives.definition: Intensity pushing a process toward a particular transition or outcome.

primitives.name: prethermal_plateau
primitives.definition: Metastable intermediate regime before full thermalization.

primitives.name: temporal_horizon
primitives.definition: Time-scale or planning window used for prediction or regulation.

primitives.name: observer
primitives.definition: Capacity to perceive or register a situation.

primitives.name: actor
primitives.definition: Capacity to take action or exert influence.

primitives.name: commitment
primitives.definition: Sustained binding to a chosen direction or intention.

primitives.name: choice
primitives.definition: Selection among multiple possible actions or paths.

primitives.name: authority
primitives.definition: Power or recognized right to act or decide.

primitives.name: intention
primitives.definition: Internal aim or purpose guiding action.

primitives.name: stance
primitives.definition: Posture toward acting — leaning toward, away from, or neutral.

primitives.name: resolution_selection
primitives.definition: Choosing which instance to resolve when only one can resolve.

primitives.name: goal_alignment
primitives.definition: Improving one goal_metric supports or does not harm others.

primitives.name: conflict_resolution_policy
primitives.definition: Rule set determining how competing goal_metrics are resolved.

primitives.name: interface
primitives.definition: Point of interaction between a system and its environment.

primitives.name: protocol
primitives.definition: Rules governing interactions across an interface.

primitives.name: state-machine
primitives.definition: Structured set of states and transitions defining system behavior.

primitives.name: model_scope
primitives.definition: Range of conditions in which a model is valid.

primitives.name: feedback
primitives.definition: Information returned to the system about effects of its actions.

primitives.name: boundary
primitives.definition: Dividing line separating system from environment.

primitives.name: boundary_integrity
primitives.definition: Condition preserving coherence of a system’s boundary.

primitives.name: input/output
primitives.definition: Channels through which information or resources enter or leave.

primitives.name: configural_cohesion
primitives.definition: Degree to which components form a unified whole.

primitives.name: local_constraint
primitives.definition: Rule applying to a specific subsystem or region.

primitives.name: constraint_decay
primitives.definition: Weakening or loss of constraints over time.

primitives.name: constraint_field
primitives.definition: Distributed pattern of constraints shaping behavior.

primitives.name: propagation_limit
primitives.definition: Maximum reach of a propagating influence.

primitives.name: constraint_saturation
primitives.definition: Point at which additional constraints no longer change behavior.

primitives.name: identity_token
primitives.definition: Stable identifier persisting across decomposition or transformation.

primitives.name: correlation_tuning
primitives.definition: Adjusting relationships between variables to improve performance.

primitives.name: correlation_locking
primitives.definition: Stabilizing relationships so they remain fixed under change.

primitives.name: energy
primitives.definition: Capacity to perform biological work or sustain life.

primitives.name: reproduction
primitives.definition: Ability to generate new organisms or copies.

primitives.name: growth
primitives.definition: Increase in size, complexity, or capacity.

primitives.name: repair_maintenance
primitives.definition: Restoration and upkeep of structure and function.

primitives.name: sensing_response
primitives.definition: Detection of stimuli and corresponding reaction.

primitives.name: stability_regulation
primitives.definition: Active management of dynamic behavior to stay within viable attractors.

primitives.name: semantic_vacuum
primitives.definition: Absence of meaning or semantic structure.

primitives.name: abstraction
primitives.definition: Removing detail to reveal higher-level structure.

primitives.name: concretization
primitives.definition: Adding detail to make an idea specific.

primitives.name: composition
primitives.definition: Combining elements into a larger whole.

primitives.name: specialization
primitives.definition: Narrowing a pattern to a specific case.

primitives.name: resolution_limit
primitives.definition: Scale at which structure becomes inaccessible.

primitives.name: irreversible_resolution
primitives.definition: A resolved state that cannot be undone.

primitives.name: degradation_drift
primitives.definition: Gradual loss of performance without optimization.

primitives.name: environmental_input
primitives.definition: Incoming conditions or signals from the environment.

primitives.name: viability_threshold
primitives.definition: Minimum condition required for continued existence.

primitives.name: viability_margin
primitives.definition: Buffer between current viability and threshold.

primitives.name: regulatory_floor
primitives.definition: Minimum regulatory action to prevent irreversible loss.

primitives.name: survival_memory
primitives.definition: Stored information about past threats and outcomes.

primitives.name: error_signal
primitives.definition: Difference between expected and actual outcomes.

primitives.name: recovery_drive
primitives.definition: Tendency to restore conditions after deviation.

primitives.name: sacrifice_threshold
primitives.definition: Point where one goal is permanently reduced to preserve another.

primitives.name: statistical_compression
primitives.definition: Reduction of effective state-space by collapsing configurations.

primitives.name: statistical_expansion
primitives.definition: Increase of effective state-space by unlocking new states.

primitives.name: exchange_phase_defect
primitives.definition: Topological alteration of identity under exchange.

primitives.name: dimensional_frustration
primitives.definition: Blocking or suppression of a natural degree of freedom.

primitives.name: dimensional_liberation
primitives.definition: Release of a previously blocked degree of freedom.
